seoTitle: YMTC Solutions | Enterprise SSD & Storage Solutions | LiTong
seoDescription: YMTC storage solutions including enterprise SSD, consumer storage, and embedded solutions. Technical support and reference designs available from authorized distributor.

faqs.question: How do I choose the right YMTC solution for my application?
faqs.answer: Selecting the right YMTC solution depends on your application requirements: (1) For enterprise data centers - choose Enterprise SSD Solution with high-capacity TLC NAND; (2) For consumer devices - choose Consumer Storage Solution with cost-effective TLC NAND; (3) For mobile applications - choose Embedded Storage Solution with low-power eMMC/UFS; (4) For automotive systems - choose Automotive Storage Solution with AEC-Q100 qualified NAND. Consider factors like capacity requirements, performance needs, endurance specifications, and environmental conditions. Contact LiTong FAE for personalized recommendations.
faqs.decisionGuide: Match solution to application requirements. Contact FAE for guidance.

faqs.question: What support does LiTong provide for YMTC solutions?
faqs.answer: LiTong provides comprehensive support for YMTC solutions: (1) Technical consultation - helping select the right NAND products for your storage application; (2) Application engineering - supporting SSD design, firmware integration, and system optimization; (3) Reference designs - providing proven design examples and evaluation platforms; (4) Quality support - handling RMA processes and quality issues; (5) Design-in support - providing technical documentation and application notes; (6) Long-term supply - ensuring stable supply for production programs. Our FAE team has expertise in NAND flash applications across enterprise, consumer, automotive, and industrial markets.
faqs.decisionGuide: LiTong provides end-to-end support from design to production for YMTC solutions.

faqs.question: Can YMTC provide custom NAND solutions?
faqs.answer: Yes, YMTC offers custom NAND solutions for specific application requirements: (1) Custom packaging - special form factors for space-constrained designs; (2) Firmware customization - optimized firmware for specific workloads; (3) Enhanced screening - additional testing for high-reliability applications; (4) Long-term supply - guaranteed availability for extended product lifecycles; (5) Automotive qualification - AEC-Q100 certification for vehicle applications. Custom solutions require minimum order quantities and development lead times. Contact LiTong to discuss your specific requirements and feasibility.
faqs.decisionGuide: Custom solutions available for volume applications. Contact LiTong to discuss requirements.

faqs.question: What is the typical lead time for YMTC solutions?
faqs.answer: YMTC solution lead times vary by complexity: (1) Standard NAND products - typically 6-8 weeks for common configurations; (2) SSD solutions - 8-12 weeks including assembly and testing; (3) Custom solutions - 12-16 weeks depending on specific requirements; (4) Volume production - 8-10 weeks for large orders. LiTong maintains strategic inventory for high-demand YMTC products. For critical projects, we recommend forecasting and buffer stock arrangements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Standard solutions 6-8 weeks, custom solutions 12-16 weeks. Contact sales for specific lead times.

faqs.question: Does YMTC offer evaluation platforms for solution development?
faqs.answer: Yes, YMTC offers evaluation platforms for solution development: (1) Reference SSD designs - complete hardware designs with BOM and layout files; (2) Firmware development kits - SDK for SSD controller integration; (3) Evaluation boards - test platforms for NAND characterization; (4) Technical documentation - detailed datasheets, application notes, and design guides; (5) Sample programs - NAND samples for prototyping and testing. Evaluation platforms are available for qualified customers. Contact LiTong sales to request evaluation resources for your project.
faqs.decisionGuide: Evaluation platforms available for qualified projects. Contact LiTong sales to request development resources.

solutions.name: Enterprise SSD Solution
solutions.title: YMTC Enterprise SSD Solution
solutions.subtitle: High-performance, high-capacity storage solution for data centers and enterprise applications
solutions.description: Complete enterprise storage solution featuring YMTC X4-9070 TLC NAND optimized for data center and enterprise server applications.
solutions.longDescription: YMTC's Enterprise SSD Solution provides a comprehensive storage platform for demanding data center and enterprise applications. The solution features the X4-9070 232-layer TLC NAND with Xtacking 4.0 architecture, delivering up to 8TB capacity in standard form factors. It enables high-performance NVMe and SATA SSD designs with up to 7GB/s sequential read and 1M IOPS random read performance. The solution is ideal for database acceleration, virtualization, cloud storage, and high-performance computing. LiTong provides complete design support including reference designs, firmware integration guidance, and qualification assistance.
benefits: Up to 8TB capacity in compact M.2 form factor

coreAdvantages.title: High Capacity
coreAdvantages.description: Up to 8TB in compact M.2 form factor

features.title: High Capacity
features.description: Up to 8TB in compact M.2 form factor

customerCases.challenge: Needed high-capacity, cost-effective storage for cloud infrastructure
customerCases.solution: Implemented YMTC-based enterprise SSDs with 8TB capacity
customerCases.results: Achieved 40% cost reduction while maintaining performance
customerCases.feedback: YMTC NAND from LiTong provided excellent value and reliability for our data center.

faqs.question: What capacity options are available for enterprise SSDs?
faqs.answer: YMTC-based enterprise SSDs are available in capacities from 1TB to 8TB in standard M.2 2280 and 2.5-inch form factors. The X4-9070 NAND enables high-density designs with up to 1Tb per die, allowing 8TB capacity in single-sided M.2 modules. For higher capacities, U.2 and E1.S form factors can support 16TB+ configurations. Contact LiTong for specific capacity and form factor options for your application.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose capacity based on application needs: 1-2TB for boot drives, 4-8TB for data storage.

faqs.question: What is the endurance rating for enterprise SSD solutions?
faqs.answer: YMTC-based enterprise SSDs offer endurance ratings from 1 to 3 DWPD (Drive Writes Per Day) depending on the specific design and over-provisioning. The X4-9070 NAND provides 5,000+ P/E cycles, enabling high-endurance designs. For example: 1 DWPD on a 4TB drive allows 4TB of writes per day for 5 years. Choose 1 DWPD for general-purpose storage, 3 DWPD for write-intensive applications like databases or virtualization.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select 1 DWPD for general use, 3 DWPD for write-intensive workloads.

faqs.question: Do enterprise SSDs include power-loss protection?
faqs.answer: Yes, YMTC-based enterprise SSDs include comprehensive power-loss protection (PLP): (1) Hardware PLP - onboard capacitors provide backup power to flush volatile caches to NAND; (2) Firmware PLP - journaling and checkpointing ensure data consistency; (3) NAND protection - program suspend/resume capabilities prevent data corruption. Our enterprise SSDs are tested with thousands of power cycle events to ensure data integrity. This protection is critical for data center applications where unexpected power events can occur.
faqs.decisionGuide: All enterprise SSDs include PLP as standard feature for data integrity.

faqs.question: What interfaces are supported for enterprise SSDs?
faqs.answer: YMTC-based enterprise SSDs support multiple interfaces: (1) PCIe Gen4 x4 NVMe - highest performance up to 7GB/s, ideal for modern data centers; (2) PCIe Gen3 x4 NVMe - widely compatible, up to 3.5GB/s; (3) SATA III - legacy compatibility, up to 550MB/s. NVMe is recommended for new designs due to superior performance and latency. SATA is suitable for legacy system upgrades. U.2, M.2, and E1.S form factors available depending on interface and capacity requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose PCIe Gen4 NVMe for new designs, SATA for legacy compatibility.

faqs.question: What kind of warranty do enterprise SSDs have?
faqs.answer: YMTC-based enterprise SSDs typically come with a 5-year warranty covering defects and endurance. Warranty terms include: (1) Time limit - 5 years from date of purchase; (2) Endurance limit - based on DWPD rating (e.g., 1 DWPD x 5 years); (3) SMART monitoring - track health and endurance usage; (4) Replacement - advance replacement options available. The warranty ensures the SSD will maintain specified performance and endurance throughout the warranty period under normal operating conditions.
faqs.decisionGuide: 5-year standard warranty with endurance coverage based on DWPD rating.

solutions.name: Consumer Storage Solution
solutions.title: YMTC Consumer Storage Solution
solutions.subtitle: Cost-effective, high-performance storage solution for consumer devices and PCs
solutions.description: Complete consumer storage solution featuring YMTC X3-9070 TLC NAND optimized for laptops, desktops, and gaming systems.
solutions.longDescription: YMTC's Consumer Storage Solution delivers high-performance, cost-effective storage for consumer applications. Based on the X3-9070 192-layer TLC NAND, this solution provides excellent performance for everyday computing, gaming, and content creation. Available in SATA and NVMe interfaces, capacities from 256GB to 4TB. The solution offers competitive pricing without compromising reliability, making it ideal for system builders, OEMs, and retail SSD brands. LiTong provides design support and stable supply for consumer storage products.

coreAdvantages.title: Fast Boot
coreAdvantages.description: Quick system startup and application launch

customerCases.results: Reduced storage cost by 25% while maintaining gaming performance
customerCases.feedback: YMTC SSDs provide excellent value for our gaming PC customers.

faqs.question: What is the difference between SATA and NVMe SSDs?
faqs.answer: SATA and NVMe SSDs differ in performance and interface: (1) SATA - up to 550MB/s, uses AHCI protocol, compatible with older systems, lower cost; (2) NVMe - up to 7,000MB/s (Gen4) or 3,500MB/s (Gen3), uses NVMe protocol designed for SSDs, requires M.2 or U.2 slot, higher performance. For new builds, NVMe is recommended. For older systems or budget builds, SATA is a good choice. Both use YMTC NAND and offer reliable storage.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose NVMe for new systems, SATA for legacy compatibility or budget builds.

faqs.question: What capacity SSD do I need for my PC?
faqs.answer: SSD capacity depends on your usage: (1) 256GB - minimum for OS and basic applications; (2) 512GB - recommended for most users, OS + applications + some games; (3) 1TB - good for gamers and content creators with moderate storage needs; (4) 2TB+ - for heavy gamers, video editors, or users with large media libraries. Consider your current storage usage and add 50% for future growth. YMTC-based SSDs are available from 256GB to 4TB to meet various needs.
faqs.decisionGuide: 512GB-1TB for most users, 2TB+ for heavy storage needs.

faqs.question: How do I migrate my data to a new SSD?
faqs.answer: Data migration to a new SSD can be done several ways: (1) Cloning software - use tools like Acronis, Macrium Reflect, or Samsung Data Migration to copy entire drive; (2) Fresh install - install OS on new SSD, reinstall applications, copy data files; (3) Backup and restore - backup to external drive, install new SSD, restore from backup. Cloning is easiest for system migration. YMTC SSDs are compatible with all major cloning software. LiTong can provide migration guidance if needed.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use cloning software for easiest migration, or fresh install for cleanest setup.

faqs.question: What is the warranty for consumer SSDs?
faqs.answer: YMTC-based consumer SSDs typically include a 3-year warranty covering manufacturing defects and specified endurance. Warranty terms: (1) Time period - 3 years from purchase; (2) TBW (Terabytes Written) - endurance limit based on capacity (e.g., 600TBW for 1TB drive); (3) Support - technical support and RMA service through LiTong; (4) Replacement - standard or advance replacement options. The warranty ensures your SSD will perform as specified throughout the warranty period under normal use.
faqs.decisionGuide: 3-year warranty with TBW endurance coverage for peace of mind.

faqs.question: Will upgrading to SSD improve my computer's performance?
faqs.answer: Upgrading from HDD to SSD provides dramatic performance improvements: (1) Boot time - reduced from minutes to seconds; (2) Application launch - 3-5x faster loading; (3) File operations - significantly faster copy and move operations; (4) Overall responsiveness - much snappier system feel. Even older computers see major improvements with SSD upgrades. For systems already using SATA SSDs, upgrading to NVMe provides additional performance gains, especially for large file operations and gaming.
faqs.decisionGuide: SSD upgrade provides the most noticeable performance improvement for any computer.
